Methodology Track Record Case Studies Library About Engage

Case study · Enterprise tier

US Market Entry — Operator Profile

The firm's domestic engine — VERIDEX BI USA — applied to operator-led US firms evaluating expansion or formation decisions. The Master's Chair Atlanta engagement is the published reference deliverable; additional engagements are listed below as they are released for publication.

Engine: VERIDEX BI — USA VERIDEX BI — USA · Engine reference
§ 01 The four anchors

How a case opens, every time.

01 Development question

For a US-domiciled firm with a clear operating thesis, where does the audit standard locate the highest-feasibility deployment of capital, time, and brand under an interstate or intra-state expansion?

02 Methodology applied

VERIDEX BI — USA, satisfying the canonical 22 gates and the four engine-level extensions. Two costed pathways, full TAM→SAM→SOM decomposition, Monte Carlo on revenue, and a separate Decision-Maker Briefing for every engagement.

03 Output

Stage-3 Full Reports, one per engagement, each with sixteen analytical outputs and a separate Decision-Maker Briefing. The Master's Chair Atlanta report is published as the engine's reference deliverable.

04 Counterfactual considered

Each engagement scores at least two named alternatives against the recommendation, with the Counterfactual Pause and Opportunity Cost Assessment that VERIDEX requires.

§ 02 The case in full

The published reference deliverable

The Master’s Chair Atlanta Stage-3 Full Report is the published reference deliverable for VERIDEX BI — USA. It is treated as the canonical example of what the engine produces when applied to an operator-led US firm under the discipline of the firm’s twenty-two-gate validation standard.

The full case study — including dual-revenue modelling, the $650M TAM decomposed to a $120M Atlanta-metro SAM, P50 five-year revenue of $5.1M with explicit confidence bands, and the interstate compliance architecture (Georgia 5.19% CIT and PIT, foreign qualification, multi-state employment law) — reads at /case-studies/masters-chair/.

Additional engagements

Additional VERIDEX BI — USA engagements are added to this page as they are released for publication. Each carries the same four constants the firm holds every deliverable to: a development question named without ambiguity, a methodology applied with the framework and gates named, an output paired with the counterfactual it rejects, and a separated Decision-Maker Briefing in its own artefact.

Firms or operators evaluating the engine for their own decision should write to admin@power-in-numbers.net under the subject line Engage · VERIDEX BI USA intake — the firm’s /engage/ page documents the five intake questions in advance.
